engrams v0.0.1

Knowledge Types

Engrams stores structured knowledge across several distinct types. Each type maps to a database table and is managed via specific engrams CLI subcommands.

Product Context

A single JSON document representing the high-level, relatively stable description of your project — goals, architecture overview, key features, and tech stack. Updated with engrams product-context update; retrieved with engrams product-context get.

When to use it: Think of this as your project's README in structured form. Update it when you make a major architectural pivot or add a significant new feature area.

Active Context

A single JSON document representing the current state of work — the present focus, open questions, next steps, and recent changes. Updated frequently during a session. Updated with engrams active-context update; retrieved with engrams active-context get.

Decisions

Individual architectural or implementation choices (ADRs) with a summary, rationale, implementation details, and optional tags. Decisions are the most commonly used knowledge type. 

Decision #7: Use JWT tokens for stateless authentication
Rationale: Scales better than sessions, works with microservices
Tags: auth, security, architecture

Supports fast keyword and phrase queries via SQLite FTS5 index: engrams decision search <query>.

Progress

Task tracking with hierarchical parent/child support. Managed via engrams progress. Each entry has a status (e.g., TODO, InProgress, Done) and an optional parent ID for subtask relationships.

System Patterns

Recurring architectural or design patterns observed or used in the project. Unlike decisions (which capture why), patterns capture how. Managed via engrams pattern. 

Pattern #3: Token validation middleware
Description: All API routes validate JWT in Authorization header
             using the shared auth.middleware module

Custom Data

A flexible category/key/value store for any project knowledge that doesn't fit the above types: glossaries, technical specifications, config values, or team guidelines. Managed via engrams custom. 

Category: config
Key: "api_host"
Value: "localhost:8080"

Supports custom value FTS search: engrams custom search <query>.

Context Links (Relationships)

Explicit edges in the knowledge graph. A link connects two entities with a named relationship type using the engrams link command:

Relationship typeMeaning
implementsPattern implements a Decision
related_toGeneral association
tracksProgress item tracks a Decision
blocksOne item blocks another
clarifiesCustom data clarifies a Decision
depends_onDependency relationship
supersedesA new decision supersedes an old one
resolvesA progress item resolves a Decision
